    print(f"  row {i}: '{line}'")

# Also check green bar region

    print(f"  row {i}: '{line}'")

</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the change! The green bar changed:
- Before: `GNNNNN` with red dot at col 31
- After: `GrNNNN` shifted left (the red dot moved to col 29, and the bar shrank from 5 wide to 3 wide)
So clicking on the green U-shape in the container caused the green bar to shrink! The green bar went from 5 cells wide to 3 cells wide, and the red dot moved from position 31 to 29.
This suggests that clicking on a U-shape "consumes" part of the matching colored bar. The green U-shape consumed 2 cells from the green bar.
Let me test this theory by clicking on another U-shape and seeing if the corresponding bar changes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The green bar shrank! Before: GNNNNN (5 N cells), After: GrNN (3 N cells)
# The red dot moved from col 31 to col 29
# Clicking on the green U-shape consumed part of the green bar

# Let me try clicking on the other green U-shape (right chamber, around row 21, col 45)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 45}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
